name: Monte Carlo Pose Uncertainty Analysis
description:
An automation integration firm is evaluating a 6-DOF robot arm for a high-precision gluing task where joint encoders have an uncertainty of ±0.005 radians. Conduct a Monte Carlo simulation in CoppeliaSim (ZMQ Remote API, port 23000) to map how Gaussian joint noise (μ=0, σ=0.005 radians) affects Tool Center Point (TCP) Euclidean deviations (in mm) across 5 distinct nominal joint configurations spanning different reach distances. Perform 100 samples per configuration by applying noise to the 6 revolute joints and measuring the resulting Cartesian position of the end-effector tip relative to the nominal position. The simulation should set joint angles directly without running dynamic physics.

Export the raw simulation data to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/mc_samples.csv including columns: config_id, sample_id, nom_j1 through nom_j6, noisy_j1 through noisy_j6, ee_x, ee_y, ee_z (in meters), and deviation_mm.

Export an aggregate report to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/uncertainty_report.json using the following structure:
{
  "end_effector_name": "/UR5/connection",
  "joint_names": ["/UR5/joint1", "/UR5/joint2", "/UR5/joint3", "/UR5/joint4", "/UR5/joint5", "/UR5/joint6"],
  "total_configurations": 5,
  "samples_per_config": 100,
  "noise_sigma_rad": 0.005,
  "results": [
    {
      "config_id": 0,
      "nominal_reach_m": 0.0,
      "max_deviation_mm": 0.0,
      "mean_deviation_mm": 0.0
    }
  ]
}
